Location prompt and quantity prompt screens will be modified to include pack/repack quantity . EX106 modifies these base screens to include a displayed pack/repack quantity above the item description . This behavior mimics the pack/repack functionality added to the EX33 RF Pack Pick Cart transaction. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Fill Active Reporting N Other N Assumptions This extension modifies the Fill Active transaction only D atabase field s ITEM_CBO.REF_FIELD5 and ITEM_CBO.REF_FIELD6 are always populated with pack/repack value respectively For EX106 RF Fill Active transaction parameters assume the following configuration: 2-2 Container Mode: iLPN entry 3-3 Item Verification Mode: Item is displayed, key user qty 11-11 Check for Capacity?: Do not check for capacity 12-12 Dynamically Assign Location?: No 15-15 Display UOM Quantity: Pack 18-18 Fill Active Mode: System directed 51-51 Single-Multi-SKU iLPN processing: None No data validations are performed on the pack/repack value s , which are pulled directly from ITEM_CBO.REF_FIELD65 and ITEM_CBO.REF_FIELD6 Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow No process flow changes in scope for this extension. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Fill Active As a part of inventory management, users have the ability to use RF Fill Active to place an iLPN into an active location . This process is modified so that pack/repack quantity is displayed throughout the transaction. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id E X106 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX106 is turned off, and no custom EX106 processing will occur. Last, the transaction specific flag for RF Fill Active must be enabled using custom transaction parameter byte 106 - 106 . This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX106 is turned off, and no custom EX106 processing will occur. Screen 2: Location Prompt WMOS modifies the field layouts in the screen per EX106 requirements. Custom field “ Pack/Repack :” is added to the existing base layout. Several other base fields are repositioned. No changes are made to any base data entry fields or control keys. Custom field “ Pack/Repack :” is added to the existing base layout. Several other base fields are repositioned. No changes are made to any base data entry fields or control keys. Reporting , Label, or Document No report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below.
